name: openspec-implement-loop
description: Implement an OpenSpec change one task at a time with fresh context per task, then a 2-models-per-angle review panel and arbiter gate. Better than openspec-implement for large multi-task changes.

verify.description: Shell command run after each task and at the final gate. Non-zero exit signals failure. Default suits pi-ext; override per project.

phases.task:
You are implementing OpenSpec change `{args.change}` ONE TASK PER ITERATION.

Initial apply-phase state (iteration 1 only; later iterations: trust tasks.md on disk):
{steps.load.output}

Prior iteration / gate feedback (empty on iteration 1):
{reflexion}

Do this now:
1. Read openspec/changes/{args.change}/tasks.md and pick the FIRST unchecked `- [ ]` task. If ALL tasks are already checked but feedback above reports problems, fix those problems instead of picking a task.
2. Read the relevant spec deltas under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/ for that task.
3. Implement ONLY that task (or the fixes). Keep the diff minimal and focused. Do not weaken or delete tests to pass.
4. Run: `{args.verify}` — if it fails, fix your change until it passes (or, if the spec is genuinely blocked/contradictory, stop and explain).
5. Flip that task's checkbox `- [ ]` -> `- [x]` in tasks.md.
6. Count how many `- [ ]` (still unchecked) tasks remain.

Output ONLY JSON (no prose): {"task":"<id/desc you just did>","remaining":<count of still-unchecked tasks>,"done":<true if remaining==0>}

run: git status --short && echo '---' && git diff HEAD | head -c 60000

phases.task:
Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

Spec-compliance review of OpenSpec change `{args.change}`.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

For EVERY requirement and scenario in the delta specs under openspec/changes/{args.change}/specs/, verify there is implementation evidence (cite file:line). Also check every task in tasks.md is genuinely done, not just checked off.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If fully compliant, output exactly: No findings.

KISS/YAGNI/DRY review of this change.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: over-engineering, needless abstractions or indirection, dead code, duplicated logic that existing code already provides, and scope creep beyond what the spec in openspec/changes/{args.change}/ asks for.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

KISS/YAGNI/DRY review of this change.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: over-engineering, needless abstractions or indirection, dead code, duplicated logic that existing code already provides, and scope creep beyond what the spec in openspec/changes/{args.change}/ asks for.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for RECALL — surface anything suspicious, including cross-file and logic-level issues; a second reviewer will filter noise.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.

Security review of this change. Optimize for PRECISION — report only findings you are confident are real and exploitable; classify each by OWASP category.

Working-tree diff (untracked files appear only in the status list — read those files yourself):
{steps.diff.output}

Hunt for: command/shell injection, unsafe eval/exec, path traversal, secret or credential leaks, missing input validation, authorization gaps (IDOR), unsafe defaults, and data exposure.

Output a numbered list of findings, each tagged [blocker], [major], or [minor]. If clean, output exactly: No findings.
